Step up your Excalidraw game

Save your drawings to the cloud. Collaborate seamlessly. Unlock additional features.

Support open-source development.

121.5k on Github

Trusted by the people & largest companies in the world

Try Excalidraw+

Sign-up
14 days of free trial
    • Excalidraw+

    Presentations

    Present your work as online slides, have real-time presentations with your guests, or export it to PPTX & PDF

    • Export presentations into PDF or PPTX

    • Have a real-time online presentations

    • Share or embed slides

    • 100 requests/day

    Extended AI

    Upgrade to 100 requests/day for the Text to diagram and Wireframe to code generative features. Custom AI workspace settings and BYOK. More features coming soon.

    • ✏️ Text to diagram

      Generate & edit diagrams using chat interface and/or the editor.

    • ▶️ Wireframe to code

      Create web page interactive prototypes and more.

    • ⚙️ Custom settings

      Custom API (BYOK). AI workspace availability management.

  • Collaborative workspace

    Manage your drawings with your team or on your own

    • 🔗 Advanced sharing

      Invite guests and manage permissions. Share embeds or slides

    • 🎤 Voice chat

      Need to discuss something quickly on the scene? Voice chat got you covered

    • 💻 Dashboard

      See what's going on within your team

  • Comments

    Perfect for detailed discussions and streamlined feedback

  • And more...

    Check it for yourself with 14 days free trial

    Missing something? Check our public roadmap

Loved by individuals

Theo - t3.gg

@t3dotgg

Of all my favorite tools, @excalidraw would be the hardest to leave behind.

I have terrible handwriting and never got to enjoy “sketching” before (scratch notes, whiteboards, etc). Excalidraw unlocked that for me and I owe y’all immensely for it ❤️❤️❤️

Guillermo Rauch

@rauchg

I’ve been really impressed about @excalidraw’s evolution.

Very high quality product that’s backed by an organic, open-source and voluntary “distributed team”

Gergely Orosz

@GergelyOrosz

It is @excalidraw! Love them. Been a paying customer since they opened up the paid tier and they are iterating quickly, making the product even better.

Andrej Karpathy

@karpathy

Yes!! :) <3 <3 <3 @excalidraw btw, really amazing and useful for graphics and diagrams, use it all the time

Mikael Lirbank

@MikaelLirbank

I just like @excalidraw so much! I use a lot of really nice tools, but Excalidraw+ probably provides me with the most delight.

I draw as I think and it really helps me organize my thoughts. And it clears my head so I can sleep without the table tennis of thoughts in my head.

Adam Wathan

@adamwathan

Excalidraw is one of my favorite tools of all time.

Made this diagram to clear up something with our electrician in literally 90 seconds!

https://excalidraw.com

Adam Gent

@Adoubleagent

I ❤️ @excalidraw

I use it all the time for several different things when working with devs, designers and other PMs.

Mainly for rough visual prototypes.

It's rough so it doesn't scare the designers. BUT still gets my point across to build a mental model with other teams.

sanjana

@SanjanaLaddha

sketching lofi on excali is just WAY better than figma

Gausden

@bgausden

Loving @excalidraw. The messy aesthetic has me focusing on what I'm trying to say and not on how it looks.

Jeff Auriemma

@JeffAuriemma

Consistently impressed by @excalidraw. I was about to manually transfer a kinda-ugly-looking Mermaid chart (another indispensable tool, btw, no shade) so I could manipulate the layout more, then discovered this banger of a feature. Saved me probably an hour of work 🙇🏻

vivek khatri

@peculiarvivek

I like writing design documents, I get to pre-think before writing any code, sort out my thoughts on implementation, and make a flow diagram which I absolutely love. I use @excalidraw a lot!

Loïs

@Vizuall.fr

Awesome product ! Collaboration is really neat. Tool is going straight to the point

Shay Davidson

@ShayHDavidson

Prototyping #gamedev ideas with @excalidraw

Kevin Kern

@kregenrek

Pretty much using @excalidraw to make all my slides for Instructa AI now.

François Fleuret

@francoisfleuret

I know this is quite weird for a TikZ fundamentalist, but I absolutely love @excalidraw. It is one of the smoothest, most intuitive GUI I have ever met if not the best.
